result:
Done. The improvement pass is complete and validated.

SUMMARY:
Discovered that `content/docs/start.mdx` ("Punto de partida") — the site's strongest decision-support asset, a 4-step triage (scope → prohibited practice → high-risk → transparency → role) with the application timeline and per-case reading routes — was orphaned: untracked in git, absent from `meta.json` (so missing from the sidebar), and unlinked from every entry page. Users could only reach it by typing the URL. This pass wired it into the information architecture as the primary entry point, a single coherent theme:
- `content/docs/meta.json`: added `start` to `pages` right after `index` so it leads the sidebar.
- `app/(home)/page.tsx`: hero primary CTA changed to "¿Te aplica el Reglamento? Empieza aquí" → `/docs/start` (was `/docs`); "Por dónde empezar" panel now leads with the triage (renumbered to 4 steps).
- `content/docs/index.mdx`: new "Empieza por aquí" section with a highlighted card, plus the triage as the new first step in "Cómo leer este portal".
- `content/docs/introduction.mdx`: triage added as the first item in "Cómo recorrerlo".
- `content/docs/summary.mdx`: brief cross-link to the triage in the header callout for readers who land directly on the summary.
- `.hermes/overnight/progress.md`: logged the pass.
Files changed: 6 modified (5 site files + progress log); `content/docs/start.mdx` remains as the pre-existing untracked page, now fully linked. No commit made (not requested).

VALIDATION:
`npm run build` passed — compiled successfully, TypeScript clean, all 71 static pages generated. Confirmed `out/docs/start/` exports and that `/docs/start/` links render in the home hero, the home panel, the sidebar, and the welcome page. Verified before editing that the page's MDX components (`Accordions`, `Steps`, `Callout`, `Cards`) are registered in `components/mdx.tsx`, and that its internal anchors resolve (`#tu-ruta-según-el-resultado` exists; Guide 01 has `### 3.4 Fechas de activación de las obligaciones` → `#34-fechas-de-activación-de-las-obligaciones`). New links use the same root-relative `/docs/...` scheme as the rest of the site (no routing regression).

NEXT_IDEA:
The triage ends with "Tu ruta según el resultado" cards, but the actual per-profile reading sequences (e.g. high-risk provider: 03 → 04 → 05 → 15) are scattered between `start.mdx` and `summary.mdx`. Next pass: build reusable reading itineraries — a dedicated "Itinerarios" page or per-profile route components (high-risk provider, deployer, transparency-only, public-sector w/ FRIA) — consistently cross-linked, turning the diagnosis into a sequenced, guide-by-guide work plan.
